Editor Login

GitHub Settings

Needs repo scope. Stored only in your browser. Never sent anywhere except GitHub's API.

First-Time Setup

Complete these steps once to get your priority board running. Takes about 5 minutes.

1

Create a GitHub account

Go to github.com and sign up for a free account if you haven't already.

2

Create a new repository

Click the + icon → New repository. Name it priority-board. Set it to Public. Check "Add a README file." Click Create.

3

Upload this HTML file

In your new repo, click Add file → Upload files. Upload this priority-board.html file. Commit it.

4

Enable GitHub Pages

Go to repo Settings → Pages. Under Source, select Deploy from a branch, branch main, folder / (root). Click Save. Your display URL will be:
https://YOUR-USERNAME.github.io/priority-board/priority-board.html

5

Create a Personal Access Token

Go to GitHub Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Tokens (classic). Click Generate new token. Give it a name, set expiration (or no expiration), and check the repo scope. Copy the token - you'll only see it once.

6

Enter your settings below

Click Done, then click Settings in the editor toolbar. Enter your GitHub username, repo name (priority-board), your token, and set an editor password.

7

Point the Cast Pro at your display URL

Use the URL from Step 4. Add ?display to the end: ...priority-board.html?display. This loads the board in display-only mode with auto-refresh.

Initializing...
